repo.one_liner: A read-only FastAPI visibility service (`api/`) that projects live status, stats, evidence, and human-gate state of an agentic-protocol engine's runs by reading `protocol.json` and on-disk `agentic-state` files over the GitHub REST API — without ever importing the engine.
repo.architecture: The service is a thin read-through projection over git-hosted state. `main.py` is the ASGI entrypoint: at import it reads `os.environ` into an immutable `Settings` (`config.py`), constructs a `GitHubClient` (`github_client.py`), and hands both to the `create_app` factory (`app.py`), which returns the FastAPI app. Every HTTP request flows: route handler → `GitHubClient` fetches raw text/tree listings from the `agentic-state` branch and protocols ref → `state_reader.py` (pure, no I/O, no engine import) parses those blobs into response dicts → Pydantic envelopes in `models.py` document the JSON shapes returned to clients. `github_client.py` wraps `httpx.Client` and normalizes upstream failures into typed exceptions (`NotFound`, `RateLimited`, `UpstreamError`) that `app.py`'s exception handlers map to 404/429/502.
repo.architecture_cont: State on disk is per-instance: an `_instance.yaml` cursor plus one `<node>.yaml` per node, with `*.evidence.json` / `*.answers.json` sidecars. `state_reader.status_projection` walks these files, groups nodes into phases (agent / fanout / gate kinds), derives per-node status from `state` plus `gates.state`, and stamps an overall status from the instance's `phase_label`. Instance identity is dual-keyed: a bare integer resolves to a `pr-<N>` instance; any other string (`ref-main`, `ui-<uuid>`) is used verbatim — so ref/UI-triggered protocols coexist with PR-keyed ones.

key_files.role: ASGI entrypoint — wires Settings → GitHubClient → create_app at import time
FastAPI factory: routes, bearer-auth dependency, protocol-name validation, exception→HTTP mapping
Pure projection layer — parses protocol.json + instance state files into status/stats/evidence/gate dicts
Synchronous httpx-based GitHub REST client; raises typed NotFound/RateLimited/UpstreamError
Frozen Settings dataclass with from_env factory — single source of runtime config
Pydantic response envelopes (ProtocolList, InstanceList, GatesResponse, etc.)
Empty package marker for the api/ service

key_behaviors: The API is strictly read-only and never imports the engine — it consumes `agentic-state` files as a data contract.
Instance addressing is dual-keyed: a bare integer path segment maps to `pr-<N>`; any other string (`ref-main`, `ui-<uuid>`) is the instance dir name verbatim.

Node status folds `state` and `gates.state`: an answered/approved gate reports `done`, an open gate reports `running`; overall status is classified from the instance's `phase_label` (✅→completed, ❌→failed, ⛔→blocked).
A fanout phase's status is failed if any leg failed, done only if all legs done, else running.
Upstream GitHub failures are normalized to typed exceptions mapped to HTTP 404/429 (with Retry-After)/502; a global NotFound handler catches blob 404s mid-assembly.
All data endpoints require a Bearer token equal to `settings.api_bearer_token`; `/healthz` is unauthenticated and returns ok/degraded.

commands.note: repo is a uv project; auto-syncs dev deps from uv.lock. Note: summarized tests target the engine, not api/ specifically
if api/ ships its own tests; unverified from summaries — confirm a tests dir exists first
main.py exposes the ASGI app; see api/README.md for required env vars

conventions: state_reader.py must stay pure: no I/O, no engine import, no GitHub calls — all fetching happens in github_client.py and is passed in as text/dicts.
Never interpolate a query-sourced protocol name into a GitHub path without `_PROTOCOL_RE` validation (single path segment; blocks `/` and `..` traversal).
Settings is frozen and built only via `Settings.from_env`; do not read os.environ elsewhere.
Route handlers raise the typed GitHub exceptions and let app.py's registered handlers map them to HTTP codes — don't hand-roll status codes for upstream errors.
New response shapes get a Pydantic envelope in models.py; keep additive fields backward-compatible (e.g. `instance` added alongside existing `pr`).
gotchas: `_pr_of` returns None for non-`pr-` instances — every response carrying `pr` must tolerate null for ref/ui-keyed runs.
A gate node's top-level `state` never leaves the gate id (e.g. `answering`) — you must read `gates.state`, not `state`, or an answered gate reports as forever-running (this was a real bug fix).

A terminal merge/`done` node (e.g. recover-mental-model's `combine`) writes no own node file; `phase` points at it but the true signal is `phase_label` on `_instance.yaml` — status_projection surfaces it on `head` so a finished run isn't reported as stuck.
`_instance.yaml` and `*.__join.yaml` are bookkeeping, not node files — `_is_node_file` filters them; adding new sidecar suffixes requires updating those ignore lists.
The engine records no timestamps in state, so `action_minutes_approx` is derived only from GitHub workflow-run wall-clock (updated_at − run_started_at) and is explicitly approximate.
`/stats` and `/gates` iterate `pr-<N>` instances only (via `_pr_numbers`); ref/ui-keyed instances are invisible to those aggregate endpoints.

api/main.py.description:
This is the ASGI entrypoint for the FastAPI visibility service. It wires together the three collaborators at import time: it reads process environment into a `Settings` object via `Settings.from_env(os.environ)`, constructs a `GitHubClient` from those settings, and passes both into `create_app` to produce the module-level `app` object that uvicorn imports and serves. All real logic lives in `api.config`, `api.app`, and `api.github_client` — this file only performs composition-root assembly.

The non-obvious contract a future editor must respect: the `app` object is built once at module import (not inside a factory function or lifespan hook), so any environment variables must be present when this module is imported, and the dependency wiring (settings → client → app) happens eagerly and in that order. The `GitHubClient` is injected into `create_app` via the `client=` keyword, meaning `create_app` accepts an already-constructed client rather than building its own — this seam exists so tests can substitute a fake client. Per the repo's architecture, this service reads engine state as a read-only data contract and must never import the engine.

data_constants: app: must remain a module-level name that uvicorn can import (e.g. api.main:app)
settings: constructed once from os.environ at import time — keep it the single source passed to both GitHubClient and create_app
edit_checklist.warning: Assembly runs at import time, so a missing/invalid env var raises during import and breaks uvicorn startup rather than surfacing as a request-time error.

historical_insights.title: Added uvicorn entrypoint
historical_insights.problem: The API had no ASGI entrypoint / documented way to run it under uvicorn.
historical_insights.root_cause: Service composition (settings, client, app) needed a single import target for the server.
historical_insights.solution: Introduced this module wiring Settings.from_env → GitHubClient → create_app into a module-level `app`, plus run docs.

capabilities: Load settings from process environment
Construct the GitHub client from settings
Assemble the FastAPI app via create_app with an injected client
Expose a module-level app for uvicorn to serve

pitfalls.description: Adding heavy or network work directly in this module body
pitfalls.consequence: It runs at import time on every uvicorn worker startup and can hang or crash before serving
pitfalls.prevention: Keep this file to pure wiring; put runtime/lifespan work inside create_app or FastAPI lifespan hooks

pitfalls.description: Letting create_app construct its own GitHubClient instead of accepting the injected client=
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks the test seam that substitutes a fake client and the single-Settings wiring
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the client= injection point; pass one Settings instance to both collaborators

pitfalls.description: Importing the engine or reading state through anything but the client
pitfalls.consequence: Violates the read-only data-contract boundary between the API and the engine
pitfalls.prevention: Only depend on api.config, api.app, api.github_client here

reading_guide.start: app — the module-level assembly line that shows the whole wiring
key: settings: it is the shared input to both collaborators
app: it is the object uvicorn serves

api/config.py.description:
Defines the immutable `Settings` dataclass and its `from_env` factory — the single source of runtime configuration for the read-only FastAPI visibility service (`api/`). It parses environment variables into a frozen dataclass: three required vars (`API_BEARER_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_REPO`) and several optional ones with defaults (`STATE_BRANCH`='agentic-state', `PROTOCOLS_REF`='main', `GITHUB_API_URL`='https://api.github.com'). The `ENGINE_WORKFLOWS` var is a comma-separated list parsed into `engine_workflows` (empty/whitespace segments dropped).

Non-obvious contracts a future editor must respect: the class is `frozen=True`, so instances are immutable — callers rely on being able to pass `Settings` around safely and compare/hash it. `from_env` validates all missing required vars at once and raises a single `ValueError` listing every missing key (test `test_from_env_reports_all_missing_required` asserts this all-at-once behavior, not fail-fast). Defaults for `STATE_BRANCH`/`PROTOCOLS_REF` align with the engine's conventions (the `agentic-state` branch and `main` protocols ref described in CLAUDE.md), so changing them silently breaks state/protocol resolution. `ENGINE_WORKFLOWS` empty string must yield `[]`, and an empty-workflows list is a meaningful signal downstream (test `test_list_workflow_runs_empty_workflows_uses_repo_runs_endpoint`).

data_constants: required tuple ('API_BEARER_TOKEN','GITHUB_TOKEN','GITHUB_REPO'): these three must stay required; adding/removing changes the ValueError contract
STATE_BRANCH default 'agentic-state': must match the engine's state branch name
PROTOCOLS_REF default 'main': must match where protocols are read from
GITHUB_API_URL default 'https://api.github.com': GitHub REST base URL
ENGINE_WORKFLOWS parsing: comma-split, strip each, drop empties → list; empty input must yield []
edit_checklist.warning: Adding a field without giving it a default (or an env fallback) will break every existing `Settings(...)` call site and `from_env` construction.

historical_insights.title: Initial FastAPI package scaffold
historical_insights.problem: No configuration surface existed for the visibility API.
historical_insights.solution: Introduced the frozen `Settings` dataclass and `from_env` factory alongside the package scaffold and health endpoint.

key_constructs.name: Settings
key_constructs.rationale: The frozen config record consumed across 6 files; its field set is the API's configuration contract, so field changes ripple widely.

key_constructs.name: from_env
key_constructs.rationale: The only sanctioned constructor from the environment; encodes the required-vars validation and default policy that tests pin.

capabilities: Parse environment variables into an immutable Settings object
Validate all required env vars and report missing ones together
Apply defaults for optional config (state branch, protocols ref, API URL)
Split ENGINE_WORKFLOWS into a cleaned list

pitfalls.description: Making `from_env` fail on the first missing required var instead of collecting all
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks `test_from_env_reports_all_missing_required`, which expects every missing key reported at once
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the list-comprehension `missing` accumulation and single ValueError listing all keys

pitfalls.description: Using `env[...]` for optional vars or forgetting the default
pitfalls.consequence: KeyError when the optional var is unset, or a wrong branch/ref used downstream
pitfalls.prevention: Use `env.get(key, default)` for optional vars and keep defaults aligned with engine conventions

pitfalls.description: Treating whitespace-only ENGINE_WORKFLOWS entries as real workflow names
pitfalls.consequence: Empty strings leak into engine_workflows, changing which GitHub runs endpoint is queried
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the `if w.strip()` filter and the top-level `.strip()` before split

pitfalls.description: Removing `frozen=True`
pitfalls.consequence: Settings becomes mutable/unhashable-differently; callers relying on immutability lose that guarantee
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the dataclass frozen

key: from_env: encodes required-var validation, defaults, and ENGINE_WORKFLOWS parsing
Settings: the frozen field list that is the config contract

api/models.py.description:
This file defines the Pydantic response envelope models for the read-only FastAPI visibility service (`api/`). It documents and validates the top-level JSON shapes returned to clients: `ProtocolList`, `InstanceList`, `GatesResponse`, and `GlobalStats`. Per the module docstring and the broader architecture, `api/` reads engine state as a data contract and never imports the engine — so these models are the boundary schema between raw state (produced as plain dicts by `state_reader`) and HTTP clients. The three list/collection envelopes deliberately wrap loosely-typed payloads (`list[dict[str, Any]]`) rather than fully modeling nested items, keeping the API tolerant of the underlying state file's evolving shape; only the outer envelope is enforced.

The non-obvious contract a future editor must respect is that `GlobalStats` is a flat, explicitly-enumerated aggregate: it splits instance counts across four disjoint process-axis buckets (`instances_running`, `instances_completed`, `instances_failed`, `instances_blocked`) plus a `_total`, and carries an approximate compute-cost pair (`action_minutes_approx` + its companion `action_minutes_note` caveat string). The `_approx`/`_note` pairing signals the number is an estimate, not a measured value — the note must travel with the number. Field names here are the wire contract; renaming any key silently breaks clients even though the models have zero in-repo references (they're consumed by external HTTP clients and populated by `state_reader`, not called from Python code).

data_constants: GlobalStats buckets: keep instances_running/completed/failed/blocked disjoint and summing to instances_total
action_minutes_approx + action_minutes_note: keep the note field whenever the approx number is present — it is the estimate caveat
InstanceList.instances: typed as list[int] (PR numbers / instance ids) — keep numeric unless the instance-key scheme changes
Envelope field names (protocols/instances/gates/by_protocol): these are the wire keys — renames break clients
edit_checklist.warning: These are the API's wire contract; renaming or retyping a field silently breaks external clients since there are no in-repo references to catch it.

historical_insights.title: Initial API response models introduced
historical_insights.problem: The visibility service needed documented, validated top-level response envelopes over the loosely-typed dicts produced by state_reader.
historical_insights.root_cause: State is read as a data contract (engine never imported), so raw dicts needed a typed HTTP boundary.
historical_insights.solution: Added the models module alongside auth, routes, and error mapping.

key_constructs.name: ProtocolList
key_constructs.rationale: Wraps the list of known protocols returned to clients; keep the `protocols` key stable.

key_constructs.name: InstanceList
key_constructs.rationale: Pairs a protocol id with its numeric instance ids; `instances` typed list[int] reflects PR-number/instance keys.

key_constructs.name: GatesResponse
key_constructs.rationale: Envelope for open human-gate entries; items stay dict[str,Any] to tolerate gate-shape evolution.

key_constructs.name: GlobalStats
key_constructs.rationale: Flat aggregate of run counts + approximate action-minutes; buckets must stay disjoint and the note must accompany the approx number.

capabilities: Define top-level HTTP response envelopes
Validate outer response shape while leaving item dicts loosely typed
Expose aggregate run statistics across process-axis buckets
Document the API-to-client data contract

pitfalls.description: Renaming a field or tightening a type on any model
pitfalls.consequence: External HTTP clients break silently — there are no Python references or mapped tests to fail
pitfalls.prevention: Treat field names/types as a versioned wire contract; coordinate with api consumers and add/adjust tests

pitfalls.description: Adding an instance-count bucket to GlobalStats without updating the aggregation source
pitfalls.consequence: instances_total no longer equals the sum of buckets, giving clients inconsistent stats
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the four buckets disjoint and ensure state_reader populates them to sum to instances_total

pitfalls.description: Dropping action_minutes_note or decoupling it from action_minutes_approx
pitfalls.consequence: Clients present an approximate compute figure as if it were measured
pitfalls.prevention: Always emit the note string together with the approx value

reading_guide.start: GlobalStats — the richest, most contract-laden model
key: GlobalStats: enumerates the disjoint instance buckets + approx/note cost pair that clients depend on
InstanceList: shows the protocol+numeric-instances pairing convention
reading_guide.skip: The three simple list envelopes (ProtocolList/GatesResponse) are structurally trivial on a first pass

api/github_client.py.description:
A synchronous GitHub REST client used by the read-only visibility API (`api/`) to fetch protocol state, evidence, and workflow-run metadata from the `agentic-state` branch without ever importing the engine. It wraps `httpx.Client` with a shared `_headers`/`_base` helper and a single `_check` method that normalizes GitHub HTTP responses into a typed exception hierarchy (`GitHubError` → `NotFound`/`UpstreamError`/`RateLimited`). It exposes tree/blob/dir listing (`list_tree`, `get_text`, `list_dir`) plus paginated workflow-run collection (`list_workflow_runs`).

Non-obvious contracts a future editor must respect: (1) `_check` treats a 403/429 as `RateLimited` ONLY when `X-RateLimit-Remaining == "0"` — a bare 403 falls through to `UpstreamError` (asserted by `test_bare_403_without_ratelimit_header_is_upstream_error`); (2) 404 is checked before the generic `>=400` branch so it maps to `NotFound` (test_get_text_404_raises_notfound); (3) `get_json = get_text` is a deliberate alias — both fetch raw content via the `application/vnd.github.raw+json` Accept header and return `.text`, so JSON parsing is the caller's job; (4) `list_tree` reads the whole tree recursively (`recursive=1`) then filters by `prefix` in-process; (5) `list_workflow_runs` pages 1–5 at `per_page=100`, stops early on a short page, maps each run down to exactly `run_started_at`/`updated_at`/`name`, and falls back to the repo-wide `/actions/runs` endpoint when `workflows` is empty (`workflows or [None]`).

data_constants: X-GitHub-Api-Version '2022-11-28': keep the pinned API version header
_check rate-limit guard: only 403/429 WITH X-RateLimit-Remaining=='0' becomes RateLimited; preserve 404-before-generic-4xx ordering
Accept headers: default 'application/vnd.github+json'; get_text uses 'application/vnd.github.raw+json' to return raw .text
list_workflow_runs pagination: per_page=100, pages range(1,6) (max 5), break on batch<100, workflows or [None] fallback
list_workflow_runs field projection: emit only run_started_at, updated_at, name
get_json = get_text alias: both return raw text, not parsed JSON
edit_checklist.warning: Changing the 404/rate-limit branch ordering or the X-RateLimit-Remaining guard in `_check` silently remaps exceptions and breaks the API route tests that depend on the exact type.

historical_insights.title: Directory enumeration added for protocol listing
historical_insights.problem: The API needed to enumerate protocols but the client could only fetch trees and blobs.
historical_insights.root_cause: Initial client (dae15ae) shipped only list_tree/get_text; protocol enumeration requires listing sub-directories.
historical_insights.solution: Added list_dir, returning sorted names of `type == 'dir'` entries from the contents endpoint.

historical_insights.title: Initial typed-error GitHub client
historical_insights.problem: API code needed structured handling of GitHub 404/403/429/5xx responses.
historical_insights.solution: Introduced GitHubClient with the GitHubError hierarchy and the _check normalizer.

key_constructs.name: RateLimited
key_constructs.rationale: Only raised on 403/429 with remaining==0; carries retry_after from the Retry-After header.

key_constructs.name: _check
key_constructs.rationale: Central response normalizer whose branch order and X-RateLimit-Remaining guard define the entire error contract callers and route tests rely on.

key_constructs.name: list_tree
key_constructs.rationale: Fetches the full recursive tree once then filters blobs by prefix in-process.

key_constructs.name: get_text
key_constructs.rationale: Most-referenced fetch (9 refs, 4 files); returns raw .text via the raw+json Accept header and is aliased as get_json.

key_constructs.name: list_dir
key_constructs.rationale: Returns sorted directory names for protocol enumeration.

key_constructs.name: list_workflow_runs
key_constructs.rationale: Bounded (5-page) paginated collector that projects runs to three fields and falls back to the repo runs endpoint when no workflows given.

related_files.relation: Primary co-change (2 shared commits); pins exception mapping, pagination, and field projection behavior.
related_files.check: That branch-ordering, the rate-limit header guard, per_page/page bounds, and the three projected run fields still match the assertions.

capabilities: Fetch and prefix-filter the state branch git tree
Retrieve raw file/blob text at a ref
List protocol sub-directories under a path
Collect and normalize paginated workflow runs
Map GitHub HTTP responses to typed exceptions

pitfalls.description: Reordering `_check` so the generic `status_code >= 400` branch runs before the 404 or rate-limit checks.
pitfalls.consequence: 404s and rate-limits get swallowed as generic UpstreamError; NotFound/RateLimited tests fail and callers lose the ability to distinguish missing state from a real upstream failure.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep 404 first, then the guarded 403/429, then the generic >=400 fallthrough.

pitfalls.description: Assuming get_text/get_json returns parsed JSON.
pitfalls.consequence: Callers get a raw string; `.get(...)` or subscripting fails at runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Remember get_json is just an alias returning .text — parse with json.loads in the caller.

pitfalls.description: Passing an empty `workflows` list expecting no requests.
pitfalls.consequence: `workflows or [None]` falls back to the repo-wide /actions/runs endpoint and still makes calls (see test_list_workflow_runs_empty_workflows_uses_repo_runs_endpoint).
pitfalls.prevention: Treat empty list as the intentional repo-wide fetch, not a no-op.

reading_guide.start: _check — it defines the error contract every other method flows through
key: get_text: raw-text fetch pattern and get_json alias
list_workflow_runs: pagination bounds and field projection

api/__init__.py.description:
This is the package-init module for the `api/` FastAPI visibility service. Its source is currently empty — a placeholder that marks `api/` as a Python package. The only substantive history is the scaffolding commit that stood up the FastAPI package, its config, and a `/healthz` endpoint; that work landed the package boundary but did not put importable code into this file itself.

The non-obvious contract a future editor must respect: per the project's core separation, the API service reads engine state as a *data contract* and must NEVER import the engine (`.github/agent-factory/engine/`). Any package-level code added here (e.g. version, exported symbols, app factory re-exports) must stay on the read-only, state-as-data side of that boundary. The test suite treats `api/__init__.py` as the anchor for the state-reader projection tests (status/stats/catalog/aggregate), so keeping the package importable and side-effect-free at import time is what those tests silently depend on.

edit_checklist.warning: Keep this package free of any engine import — the API must consume state only as a data contract.

historical_insights.title: Initial FastAPI package scaffold
historical_insights.problem: No API package existed to expose engine run state.
historical_insights.root_cause: New read-only visibility service needed a Python package boundary, config, and a liveness endpoint.
historical_insights.solution: Scaffolded the FastAPI package with config and a /healthz endpoint; this file was created as the package init.

capabilities: Marks api/ as a Python package
Anchors the read-only visibility service package
Keeps API decoupled from the engine

pitfalls.description: Adding import-time side effects or heavy imports (esp. importing the engine) into this init module.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks the API's read-only data-contract boundary and can fail/slow the state-reader test modules that import the package.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep `__init__.py` minimal; put logic in submodules and never import from `.github/agent-factory/engine/`.

reading_guide.start: The file is empty — read api/README.md and the CLAUDE.md 'api/' description for the package's role.
reading_guide.skip: This file itself; the substance lives in api/ submodules and tests/api/.

api/app.py.description:
This module builds the FastAPI application for the read-only Protocol Visibility API via the `create_app` factory. It exposes endpoints to list protocols, list PR instances, and project per-instance status/stats/evidence/gates over state files it fetches through an injected GitHub client (never importing the engine — state is read as a data contract). All read logic is delegated to `state_reader`; this file is routing, auth, input validation, instance-id resolution, and error mapping. Exception handlers map the client's `NotFound`/`RateLimited`/`UpstreamError` to 404/429 (honoring `Retry-After`)/502, so a `NotFound` escaping mid-assembly still yields 404 globally.

Non-obvious contracts a future editor must respect: (1) protocol names are query/path-sourced and MUST be validated with `_PROTOCOL_RE` (via `_validate_protocol`) before interpolation into any GitHub API path — this blocks path traversal (`..`, `/`); `_proto_json` and `_instance_files` both enforce it. (2) `_resolve_instance` treats a bare-digit ident as `pr-<N>` (back-compat) and any other string (e.g. `ref-main`, `ui-<uuid>`) verbatim — instance dirs are never bare digits, keeping this unambiguous. (3) `require_auth` guards every route except `/healthz`, which is deliberately open and returns `{status: ok|degraded}` never raising. (4) The client is injected and read per-request via `cl(request)` (from `request.app.state.client`) so tests can swap a fake; `_instance_files` requires `_instance.yaml` to exist or it 404s.

data_constants: PROTO_DIR: must stay '.github/agent-factory/protocols' — used to list/fetch protocol.json against protocols_ref
_PROTOCOL_RE: keep '^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]*$' anchored (no '/' or '..') — it is the path-traversal guard
MINUTES_NOTE: the disclaimer string returned as action_minutes_note in /stats
edit_checklist.warning: Any new endpoint that interpolates a protocol/instance name into a client path must call _validate_protocol first, or you reopen path traversal.

historical_insights.title: Path traversal via query-sourced protocol name
historical_insights.problem: The ?protocol= param (and path segments) could contain '/' or '..' and traverse out of the protocols/state dirs when interpolated into GitHub API paths.
historical_insights.root_cause: Query-sourced names were used unvalidated in client path interpolation.
historical_insights.solution: Added _PROTOCOL_RE validation (^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]*$) enforced in _proto_json and _instance_files, returning 400 on mismatch.

historical_insights.title: Non-PR ref-targeted instances (UI workflow_dispatch)
historical_insights.problem: The MM recovery protocol needed a non-PR entry point keyed by ref/UI id, but routes assumed a bare PR number.
historical_insights.solution: _resolve_instance maps bare digits to pr-<N> and passes other idents (ref-main, ui-<uuid>) verbatim; evidence route adds additive `instance` id alongside nullable `pr`.

historical_insights.title: Bulk node-keyed evidence endpoint
historical_insights.problem: Needed a single endpoint returning all node evidence + answers for an instance.
historical_insights.solution: Added /instances/{ident}/evidence delegating to state_reader.evidence_projection, returning protocol/pr/instance plus the projection.

key_constructs.name: PROTO_DIR
key_constructs.rationale: Base repo path for all protocol.json lookups against protocols_ref; changing it moves every protocol fetch.

key_constructs.name: MINUTES_NOTE
key_constructs.rationale: The approximation disclaimer returned in /stats action_minutes_note.

key_constructs.name: _PROTOCOL_RE
key_constructs.rationale: The single regex enforcing that protocol names are one safe path segment — the traversal guard.

key_constructs.name: create_app
key_constructs.rationale: The app factory holding all routes, auth, validation, instance resolution, and exception handlers; the sole public entry.

related_files.relation: Co-changes with this file (3 shared commits); exercises routing, auth, validation, and error mapping.
related_files.check: Update/verify tests for traversal->400, unknown protocol->404, gates, instance status/evidence, and NotFound->404 mapping.

capabilities: Build the FastAPI app and inject a GitHub client
Authenticate requests with a bearer token (except /healthz)
Validate protocol names against path traversal
Resolve PR-number vs ref/UI instance ids
Map client errors to 404/429/502 and project status/stats/evidence/gates

pitfalls.description: Adding a route that fetches a protocol/instance without calling _validate_protocol (directly or via _proto_json/_instance_files).
pitfalls.consequence: Reintroduces path-traversal on query/path-sourced names.
pitfalls.prevention: Route all name-based fetches through _proto_json / _instance_files, or call _validate_protocol explicitly.

pitfalls.description: Assuming ident is always a PR number and formatting it as pr-<ident>.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks ref-/UI-keyed instances (ref-main, ui-<uuid>).
pitfalls.prevention: Always resolve via _resolve_instance and preserve nullable pr / additive instance in responses.

pitfalls.description: Making /healthz require auth or letting it raise.
pitfalls.consequence: Health probes fail; endpoint is contractually open and must return ok/degraded only.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep healthz out of require_auth and keep its broad try/except returning degraded.

pitfalls.description: Importing or depending on the engine to read state.
pitfalls.consequence: Violates the read-only data-contract boundary between API and engine.
pitfalls.prevention: Read state only through the client + state_reader projections.

reading_guide.start: create_app — everything (routes, auth, validation, error mapping) is defined inside it.
key: _validate_protocol/_PROTOCOL_RE: the security boundary every fetch depends on
_resolve_instance: how pr-N vs ref-/ui- instances are addressed
exception handlers (_nf/_rl/_up): the NotFound->404 / RateLimited->429 / UpstreamError->502 contract
reading_guide.skip: The individual state_reader delegations (status_projection, instance_stats, evidence_projection) — logic lives in state_reader, not here.

api/state_reader.py.description:
state_reader.py is the pure read-only projection layer for the FastAPI visibility service (`api/`). It parses protocol.json blobs and on-disk `agentic-state` instance files (`_instance.yaml` + per-node `.yaml` + `.evidence.json`/`.answers.json` sidecars) into JSON views: a protocol catalog (`list_protocols`/`protocol_detail`), a live run status tree (`status_projection`), per-instance stats (`instance_stats`), evidence/answers split (`evidence_projection`), gate views (`gate_view`), and action-minute totals (`sum_run_minutes`). It reads the engine's state as a data contract and never imports the engine, so it must track the engine's file/YAML conventions by hand rather than by shared code.

The load-bearing non-obvious contracts: (1) instance keys are `pr-<N>` OR ref-/ui-keyed, so `_pr_of` returns None for non-PR runs and every projection tolerates a null pr. (2) A gate node's top-level `state` stays frozen at the gate id (e.g. `answering`) for its whole life; real progress lives in `gates.state` (open→answered/approved/rejected), so `_node_status` MUST read `gates.state` first or answered gates read 'running' forever. (3) A terminal `merge`/`combine` node writes no own file and leaves `_instance.yaml` `phase` pointing at it, signaling done only via `phase_label: "✅ done"` — so status must fall back to `classify_label(phase_label)` for a head with no node file. (4) Node files are distinguished from sidecars/bookkeeping via `_is_node_file` (excludes `_instance.yaml`, `.__join.yaml`, and non-`.yaml`), and process status (done/failed/running) is kept orthogonal to verdict.

data_constants: STATE_FILE_SUFFIX '.yaml': every node-file gate and stem strip depends on it; keep all suffix arithmetic consistent
EVIDENCE_SUFFIX/ANSWERS_SUFFIX '.evidence.json'/'.answers.json': evidence_projection splits sidecars by these; must match engine's sidecar naming
_IGNORE_FILES ('_instance.yaml',) and _IGNORE_SUFFIXES ('.__join.yaml',): the only non-node .yaml exclusions — add any new bookkeeping file here or it pollutes phases/stats
_node_status gate vocabulary: answered/approved→done, rejected/failed→failed, else running — keep in sync with engine gates.state values
classify_label markers: '✅'/'done'→completed, '❌'/'failed'→failed, '⛔'/'blocked'→blocked, else running — must match phase_label emoji the engine stamps
edit_checklist.warning: Reading a gate node's top-level `state` instead of `gates.state` reintroduces the answered-gate-forever-running bug (39a1293).

historical_insights.title: Answered/approved gates reported as forever-running
historical_insights.problem: The visibility API showed the interactive protocol's socratic answering gate (and code-review's approval gate after /approve) as running even after the run completed.
historical_insights.root_cause: A gate node's top-level `state` stays the gate id (e.g. 'answering') for its whole life; progress lives in `gates.state`. `_node_status` only inspected top-level `state`, so an answered gate always read 'running'.
historical_insights.solution: Made `_node_status` gate-aware: gates.state answered/approved→done, rejected/failed→failed, else running.

historical_insights.title: Terminal merge/combine run reported as stuck
historical_insights.problem: A completed merge node (recover-mental-model's `combine`) made a finished run look stuck at `combine` with no head status/kind.
historical_insights.root_cause: The merge node writes no own state file and the engine leaves `_instance.yaml` `phase` pointing at it, signaling completion only via `phase_label: '✅ done'`; status_projection matched `phase` against on-disk node files so the head came back statusless.
historical_insights.solution: Added top-level instance `status` via `classify_label(phase_label)` and, when the head has no node file but reached terminal, surfaced done/failed on the head.

historical_insights.title: Head could not distinguish stale vs fresh terminal done
historical_insights.problem: The head was {phase, kind, status} with no run identity, so a stale terminal `done` was indistinguishable from a fresh one.
historical_insights.root_cause: Run identity (head_sha, run_id, attempt) existed in state but was not surfaced.
historical_insights.solution: Surface head.head_sha always, head.run_id/attempt for single agent heads, and run_id on every agent leaf via `_leaf_view`/`_run_id_of`.

historical_insights.title: Path-traversal via ?protocol= and leaf-branch flattening
historical_insights.problem: Query-sourced protocol id could enable path traversal; fanout leaf branches were flattened to just id+workflow.
historical_insights.root_cause: Protocol id unvalidated (validation added in callers _proto_json/_instance_files, not this file); `_state_summary` `keep` list lacked 'workflow' for leaf branches.
historical_insights.solution: Added 'workflow' to `_state_summary` keep so leaf branches retain workflow/max_iterations/checks; protocol-id regex validation added upstream.

historical_insights.title: Sidecars/join markers miscounted as node files
historical_insights.problem: Non-node .yaml/JSON files risked being counted as phases; dead code and unused import lingered.
historical_insights.root_cause: Sidecar exclusion was incomplete / based on dead constants.
historical_insights.solution: Introduced `_is_node_file` using STATE_FILE_SUFFIX + _IGNORE_FILES + _IGNORE_SUFFIXES; strengthened tests injecting real sidecar/join filenames.

historical_insights.title: Faked absent optional catalog fields
historical_insights.problem: Catalog emitted version="" and max_depth:None instead of omitting missing optional fields.
historical_insights.root_cause: Defaults were faked rather than omitted.
historical_insights.solution: Only include version/max_depth when present in the protocol.

key_constructs.name: _pr_of
key_constructs.docstring: PR number for a `pr-<N>` instance, else None (ref-/ui-keyed runs have no PR).
key_constructs.rationale: Guards the PR-optional invariant so ref-/ui-keyed runs project a null pr instead of failing.

key_constructs.name: _state_summary
key_constructs.rationale: Recursively summarizes protocol node graph; keep list must include 'workflow' so leaf branches aren't flattened.

key_constructs.name: _is_node_file
key_constructs.rationale: The gate separating real node state files from sidecars/join markers/_instance.yaml; every phase/stat loop relies on it.

key_constructs.name: _node_status
key_constructs.rationale: Single source of truth for a node's process status; gate-aware (gates.state) before top-level state — regressions here break every gated protocol's reported status.

key_constructs.name: _run_id_of
key_constructs.docstring:
The run that produced the node's latest attempt (history[-1].agent_run_id),
or None for a node with no agent run yet (e.g. a gate).

key_constructs.name: _leaf_view
key_constructs.rationale: Uniform agent-leaf shape (status/iterations/run_id/checks) reused for agent phases and fanout branches.

key_constructs.name: status_projection
key_constructs.rationale: The central view (11 refs, 4 files) building phases + head; must handle single/fanout/gate kinds and fileless terminal heads.

key_constructs.name: classify_label
key_constructs.rationale: Maps engine phase_label emoji/words to the completed/failed/blocked/running vocabulary used across instance and head status.

key_constructs.name: sum_run_minutes
key_constructs.rationale: Aggregates GitHub run durations via _parse_iso; tolerant of missing/invalid timestamps.

related_files.relation: Primary status/gate/head-identity coverage; co-changes on nearly every status fix (5 shared commits)
related_files.check: Update assertions for gate-aware status, null-pr, fileless terminal heads, and run identity when changing _node_status/status_projection

related_files.relation: Documents the projection contract and caveats (e.g. deep-nested head.kind/status)
related_files.check: Keep documented status vocabulary and head caveats in sync with classify_label / status_projection behavior

related_files.relation: Covers list_protocols/protocol_detail omission of absent optional fields
related_files.check: Verify version/max_depth omission and sort order if you change catalog builders
Confirm phase counting and iteration totals still match after status/node-file changes

capabilities: project engine state files into a status tree with head run identity
classify instance/phase status from phase_label and gates.state
split instance sidecars into evidence and answers maps
summarize protocol catalog and per-node state graph
compute per-instance stats and action-minute totals

pitfalls.description: Reading node completion from top-level `state` for gate nodes
pitfalls.consequence: Answered/approved gates report 'running' forever (regression of 39a1293)
pitfalls.prevention: Always consult `gates.state` first in `_node_status`; keep the answered/approved/rejected mapping intact

pitfalls.description: Assuming every instance has a PR number
pitfalls.consequence: ref-/ui-keyed recover-mental-model runs crash or mislabel; test_status_projection_ref_instance_has_null_pr fails
pitfalls.prevention: Route all PR extraction through `_pr_of`, which returns None for non-`pr-<N>` keys

pitfalls.description: Building head status only from on-disk node files
pitfalls.consequence: Terminal merge/combine heads (no own file) look statusless/stuck (regression of 7751ce1)
pitfalls.prevention: Fall back to `classify_label(phase_label)` and surface done/failed on a fileless head that reached terminal

pitfalls.description: Adding a new bookkeeping .yaml without excluding it
pitfalls.consequence: It gets counted as a phase/node in status and stats
pitfalls.prevention: Add it to _IGNORE_FILES or _IGNORE_SUFFIXES so `_is_node_file` filters it

reading_guide.start: status_projection — the assembly point that ties instance file, nodes, gates, fanout, and head together
key: _node_status: encodes the gate-vs-top-level-state contract every status view depends on
_is_node_file: defines what counts as a node file vs sidecar/bookkeeping
classify_label: the terminal-status fallback for fileless heads
_pr_of: the PR-optional invariant for ref-/ui-keyed runs
reading_guide.skip: sum_run_minutes / _parse_iso (independent action-minute helpers) on a first pass
